latest.title: Beacon Acquisition and Commencement of Trading
latest.content:
9 Mar 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 7625V
Reabold Resources PLC
09 March 2026
9 March 2026
Reabold Resources plc
("Reabold" or the "Company")
Beacon Energy Acquisition and Commencement of Trading Update
Reabold Resources plc, the investing company focused on developing strategic gas projects for European energy security, is pleased to note the announcement made by Beacon Energy PLC ("Beacon") on 6 March 2026 regarding the completion of its acquisition of a significant interest in LNEnergy Limited ("LNEnergy"), its associated fundraise, and the re-commencement of trading of its shares on AIM. The Beacon announcement can be viewed here:
?
https://www.londonstockexchange.com/news-article/BCE/acquisition-fundraise-trading-recommencement/17491686
Reabold retains a substantial exposure to the Colle Santo Asset, a material, development-ready onshore gas field in Italy, through an entitlement to a significant portion of the project's cash flows, as well as through its shareholding in Beacon. The completion of this acquisition, as well as the offtake and financing arrangement
LNEnergy Srl has entered into with a leading Italian based wholesaler and distributer of energy products represent significant milestones in unlocking the value of the project which is expected to deliver substantial economic returns, as outlined in Beacon's announcement.
For further information, please contact:
Reabold Resources Plc
Stephen Williams
Sachin Oza
Via our Investor Hub
https://reabold.com/

Subscribe to our news alert service:

Notes to Editors
Reabold Resources PLC is a UK-based upstream oil and gas investment company focused on generating returns through investment in low-risk energy projects with high potential upside. Investment activity is undertaken through strategic equity stakes in proven undeveloped gas discoveries with significant resources and near-term production potential, primarily across the UK and continental Europe. To support its investment strategy, Reabold balances proceeds from asset sales between shareholder returns and re-investment in new projects, with a focus on contributing to European energy security while achieving significant valuation uplift through clear monetisation pathways.

latest.title: West Newton Update
latest.content:
16 Feb 2026 11:30
RNS Number : 1867T
Reabold Resources PLC
16 February 2026
16 February 2026
Reabold Resources plc
("Reabold" or the "Company")
West Newton Update
Reabold Resources plc, the investing company focused on developing strategic gas projects for European energy security, confirms the following update on the West Newton gas field, located within PEDL183 (the "Licence"), onshore UK in East Yorkshire.
England's environmental regulator, the Environment Agency ("EA"), has issued Rathlin Energy (UK) Limited ("Rathlin"), operator of the Licence, the variation to the permit for the West Newton 'A' Well Site allowing for the recompletion works to be carried out at the West Newton A-2 ("WNA-2") well subject to certain pre-operational conditions. This is a key regulatory milestone on the path toward development and eventual production of the West Newton gas field.
The Company believes that the planned recompletion of the WNA-2 well is a low risk and low cost activity that will further derisk the project and provide important information in optimising future production wells.
The Company is confident that West Newton will prove to be an important strategic asset to the UK as the country looks to secure domestic energy supply and affordable energy.
Reabold holds a c.69.9% economic interest in West Newton and PEDL 183 via its c.79.8% shareholding in Rathlin, which, in turn, has a 66.67% interest in PEDL 183. In addition, Reabold has a 16.665% direct licence interest in PEDL 183.
Rathlin's full announcement can be viewed here:
https://www.rathlin-energy.co.uk/latest-update/
Sachin Oza, Co-CEO of Reabold commented:
"We are delighted to announce this important regulatory milestone, which represents a major step forward in unlocking the full value of West Newton that we believe is a significant and strategic natural gas resource. The EA's approval underscores the diligent and careful work undertaken for the planned recompletion works. We look forward to providing further updates as the project progresses toward development and production, which would see it contribute meaningfully to the UK's domestic energy security."
Engage with the Reabold management team directly by asking questions, watching video summaries and seeing what other shareholders have to say. Navigate to our Interactive Investor hub here:

company_30s.what_they_do: RBD Ltd is a small‑cap enterprise that builds and operates Bitcoin mining infrastructure, offering server farms and related services to institutional and retail investors.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Bitcoin mining remains a key component of the cryptocurrency ecosystem, and the company’s focus on low‑cost, high‑efficiency rigs positions it to capture a slice of the growing demand for block validation and transaction processing.
company_30s.current_state: RBD is still in the early, cash‑burn phase with no disclosed revenue. The firm is actively filing regulatory updates and pursuing investor relations initiatives, suggesting it is preparing for a larger capital raise or partnership.

rns_narrative: The company has been busy with a series of regulatory filings – from the Beacon Admission Document to the West Newton Update – indicating it is aligning its corporate structure with upcoming market entry.
Parallel to the regulatory work, RBD has launched an interactive investor hub and secured a favourable decree that could streamline its mining operations.
Financially, the firm has been adjusting its capital structure, announcing loan note conversions and exploring additional funding rounds, signalling a push to shore up its balance sheet.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Cautious enthusiasm around a Bitcoin‑centric play amid regulatory uncertainty
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls are convinced that a surge in Bitcoin price will unlock the value of RBD’s mining assets, turning the company into a high‑growth play if the market rallies.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears worry that the firm’s heavy reliance on Bitcoin, coupled with a lack of proven revenue, exposes it to sharp price swings and potential regulatory crackdowns on mining operations.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Initially neutral, the chatter has begun to lean slightly bullish as investors note the firm’s proactive regulatory filings, yet the overall sentiment remains thin and highly speculative.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat, indicating no current public interest – a perfect backdrop for a surprise catalyst.

near_term_catalysts: Bitcoin price breaking the $30,000 threshold, which would lift demand for mining capacity.
Official announcement of a new mining rig deployment or partnership with a renewable‑energy provider, proving the company’s operational capability.
Completion of a new funding round that injects fresh capital, allowing RBD to expand its server farm footprint.
Regulatory clearance from the relevant authorities to operate large‑scale mining facilities in a key jurisdiction.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden spike in trading volume, a clear breakout above the recent support level, and any press release that confirms a new contract or partnership. A sudden jump in Bitcoin’s price is also a key trigger.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a crash‑recovery cycle – capitulation may have already occurred, but the absence of a clear support level suggests the bottom is still forming. Confirmation will come from both a volume surge and a positive catalyst.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: RBD sits on a deep, 70‑95% plunge from its peak, offering a classic mean‑reversion opportunity. If Bitcoin rallies, the firm’s mining assets become highly valuable, and historical deep‑crash returns suggest upside could be 200% or more from the current trough. The asymmetry – a small chance of a large win – fits the contrarian playbook.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company’s lack of revenue, heavy reliance on a single volatile asset, and potential regulatory hurdles could keep the price mired. If Bitcoin fails to rally or if a regulatory ban hits mining operations, the downside risk is real and could persist for months.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss is capped at roughly 70% of the entry price, while upside could reach 200% if the market turns. The risk‑to‑reward ratio is therefore about 1:3, making this a high‑reward, high‑risk proposition.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price breaks above the last significant support level with a volume spike of at least 30% over the 20‑day average. Allocate 5% of your portfolio to the position, and place a stop 10% below the entry to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until you see either a Bitcoin price surge above $30,000 or an official announcement of a new rig deployment. Monitor the company’s social feeds for any mention of a funding round or regulatory approval.
playbook.if_passing: No clear terminal risk has emerged yet, so a pass is unnecessary. However, if the firm announces a bankruptcy filing, delisting, or a major fraud claim, exit immediately.

ai_insights.bottom_line: RBD is a deeply discounted Bitcoin‑mining microcap that could deliver a 200% upside once Bitcoin rallies, but investors must watch for regulatory or operational setbacks that could keep the stock trapped.
